children.heading: Controlled
children.label: 1 Controlled
children.text: The term “controlled” refers to an item subject to the jurisdiction of the United States under subchapter I.

children.heading: Dual-use
children.label: 2 Dual-use
children.text: The term “dual-use”, with respect to an item, means the item has civilian applications and military, terrorism, weapons of mass destruction, or law-enforcement-related applications.

children.label: A
children.text: the shipment or transmission of the item out of the United States, including the sending or taking of the item out of the United States, in any manner; and

children.label: B
children.text: the release or transfer of technology or source code relating to the item to a foreign person in the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: the Export Administration Regulations as promulgated, maintained, and amended under the authority of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act [ et seq.] and codified, as of , in subchapter C of chapter VII of title 15, Code of Federal Regulations; or50 U.S.C. 1701August 13, 2018

children.label: B
children.text: regulations that are promulgated, maintained, and amended under the authority of subchapter I on or after .August 13, 2018

children.label: A
children.text: any natural person who is not a lawful permanent resident of the United States, citizen of the United States, or any other protected individual (as such term is defined in );section 1324b(a)(3) of title 8

children.label: B
children.text: any corporation, business association, partnership, trust, society or any other entity or group that is not incorporated in the United States or organized to do business in the United States, as well as international organizations, foreign governments and any agency or subdivision of a foreign government (e.g., diplomatic mission).

children.heading: In-country transfer
children.label: 6 In-country transfer
children.text: The term “in-country transfer”, with respect to an item subject to controls under subchapter I, means a change in the end-use or end user of the item within the same foreign country.

children.heading: Item
children.label: 7 Item
children.text: The term “item” means a commodity, software, or technology.

children.label: A
children.text: a natural person;

children.label: B
children.text: a corporation, business association, partnership, society, trust, financial institution, insurer, underwriter, guarantor, and any other business organization, any other nongovernmental entity, organization, or group, or any government or agency thereof; and

children.label: C
children.text: any successor to any entity described in subparagraph (B).

children.label: A
children.text: the shipment or transmission of the item from a foreign country to another foreign country, including the sending or taking of the item from the foreign country to the other foreign country, in any manner; and

children.label: B
children.text: the release or transfer of technology or source code relating to the item to a foreign person outside the United States.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 10 Secretary
children.text: Except as otherwise provided, the term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Commerce.

children.heading: Technology
children.label: 11 Technology
children.text: The term “technology” includes information, in tangible or intangible form, necessary for the development, production, or use of an item.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 12 United States
children.text: The term “United States” means the several States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, American Samoa, Guam, the United States Virgin Islands, and any other territory or possession of the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: any individual who is a citizen or national of the United States or who is an individual described in subparagraph (B) of ;section 1324b(a)(3) of title 8

children.label: ii
children.text: a corporation or other legal entity which is organized under the laws of the United States, any State or territory thereof, or the District of Columbia; and

children.label: iii
children.text: any person in the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: for purposes of subchapter II, any United States resident or national (other than an individual resident outside the United States and employed by other than a United States person), any domestic concern (including any permanent domestic establishment of any foreign concern) and any foreign subsidiary or affiliate (including any permanent foreign establishment) of any domestic concern which is controlled in fact by such domestic concern, as determined under regulations by the Secretary.

children.heading: Weapons of mass destruction
children.label: 14 Weapons of mass destruction
children.text: The term “weapons of mass destruction” means nuclear, radiological, chemical, and biological weapons and delivery systems for such weapons.
